steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which description of vocal manipulation in hyperpop is best supported by the text. The passage ends by stating that hyperpop “invites the listener to reflect on the extent to which digital technology mediates the human experience today.” That closing sentence carries the passage’s main claim about what vocal manipulation accomplishes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "It serves as an example of digital technology’s negative influence on daily life.": The passage says technology mediates human experience — it never calls that influence negative. ✗
  • "It represents the continuity of human experience despite social and historical change.": The passage says nothing about historical continuity; the manipulation is tied to the present, not a timeless pattern. ✗
  • "It functions as a commentary on contemporary social conditions.": Prompting reflection on digital technology’s role in modern life is exactly a commentary on contemporary conditions. ✓
  • "It symbolizes the power of contemporary music to influence listeners’ sense of self.": The passage focuses on reflecting on technology’s mediation, not on shaping listeners’ identity. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
